architecture.summary: GreatGramma is a grammar-constrained decoding (GCD) system that prevents LLMs from generating outputs violating a context-free grammar (CFG). It operates in two phases: (1) offline preprocessing constructs a token-spanner table by analyzing how the LLM's subword tokenizer aligns with CFG terminals defined as regular expressions, building a lexer FSA and mapping LLM tokens to sequences of terminals; (2) online decoding uses this table to compute boolean masks over the LLM vocabulary at each step, masking tokens that provably cannot lead to valid parses. The core innovation addresses token misalignment—LLM tokens may span multiple, partial, or no CFG terminals—by precomputing a lexer-state-dependent mapping.

claims.statement: GreatGramma achieves an average 17.71x speedup in offline preprocessing compared to related approaches
GreatGramma maintains state-of-the-art online masking efficiency of 5-32ms per token
Existing GCD algorithms require tens of minutes to preprocess common grammars
GCD implementations from existing published papers contain soundness bugs (e.g., they mask tokens that should not be masked or do not terminate when preprocessing simple grammars)
The key challenge with implementing GCD algorithms is that the tokens used by subword tokenizers in LLMs do not align with the terminals used by parsers
Grammar-constrained decoding can guarantee that LLM outputs match specified context-free grammar rules by masking out tokens that will provably lead to outputs not in the grammar
